name: Tour of the Monasteries of La Safor and La Vall d'Albaida
name_original: Tour por los Monasterios de La Safor y La Vall d'Albaida
region_intro: Touring cycling around Rótova offers a diverse landscape, transitioning from an alluvial plain in the southeast to more mountainous terrain in the northwest. The region is characterized by irrigated crops, predominantly orange groves, along the Vernisa River, providing picturesque scenery. Further inland, the topography includes higher elevations such as Cerro del Agua and Alto de las Águilas, contributing to varied cycling experiences. The Serpis River is a central natural feature, with sections of greenways offering impressive views. Best touring cycling routes around Rótova The most popular touring cycling route is Tour of the Monasteries of La Safor and La Vall d'Albaida, a 23.7 miles (38.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 44 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the cultural heritage of the region. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Port de La Drova Climb – La Visteta viewpoint loop from Camí Pinet, a difficult 35.7 miles (57.5 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and offers expansive views of the surrounding landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Large mural by Toni Espinar – Park in front of the church loop from Ròtova, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail leading through local villages and fields, often completed in about 34 minutes. Touring cycling around Rótova is defined by its mix of flat orange groves, river valleys, and challenging mountainous hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to demanding climbs. The routes in Rótova are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 370 reviews. More than 5000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Rótova's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Rótova?
A: Rótova offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You'll find a mix of flat alluvial plains, especially in the southeast with picturesque orange groves, and more mountainous, elevated terrain as you move northwest. The region features river valleys, such as along the Serpis River, and challenging climbs in areas like Cerro del Agua and Alto de las Águilas, providing varied experiences for all skill levels.

Q: Are there many touring cycling routes available in Rótova?
A: Yes, Rótova boasts a comprehensive network for touring cyclists. There are over 130 routes, ranging from easy, leisurely rides through fields and villages to more challenging ascents in the mountainous areas.

Q: What are some family-friendly touring cycling options in the Rótova area?
A: For families, the region offers accessible and mostly flat routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40647934" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Large mural by Toni Espinar – Park in front of the church loop from Ròtova&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.3-mile (8.6 km) trail leading through local villages and fields. Additionally, the Greenway of Safor (Vía Verde de la Safor) is a paved, mostly flat path ideal for families, traversing citrus groves and featuring picnic areas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Rótova?
A: Yes, many routes in the Rótova area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example of a challenging circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33762428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de La Drova Climb – La Visteta viewpoint loop from Camí Pinet&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 35.7 miles (57.5 km) and offers expansive views.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I visit while cycling in Rótova?
A: Rótova is rich in history. Just 1 km from the town, you can visit the impressive &lt;strong&gt;Monastery of San Jeronimo de Cotalba&lt;/strong&gt;, a Gothic-Renaissance monastery. The region also features remnants of a Muslim fortress at the Ruins of Borró Castle and traditional Riuraus. While cycling, you might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6235362" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villa Bota&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4014378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Bastida Refuge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or scenic viewpoints can I discover on a touring bike in Rótova?
A: The Serpis River is a central natural feature, with sections of greenways offering impressive views. You can also explore the picturesque orange groves along the Vernisa River. For stunning vistas, consider routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3602679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Central viewpoint of the Borrons marsh&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5968459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Duke's Pond Spring&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Rótova?
A: The Mediterranean climate makes Rótova pleasant for outdoor activities for much of the year. Spring is particularly beautiful when the orange groves are in bloom, and autumn offers comfortable temperatures for cycling. These seasons generally provide ideal weather conditions for exploring the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any specific greenways or dedicated cycle paths in Rótova?
A: Yes, the region is home to notable greenways. The &lt;strong&gt;Racó del Duc Route (Serpis Greenway)&lt;/strong&gt; follows the Serpis River through its canyon, offering impressive views. Another excellent option is the &lt;strong&gt;Greenway of Safor (Vía Verde de la Safor)&lt;/strong&gt;, a paved cycle path connecting Gandía with Oliva, known for its flat terrain and scenic passage through citrus groves.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Rótova?
A: The touring cycling routes in Rótova are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 370 reviews. Cyclists often praise the mix of flat orange groves and challenging mountainous hills, the quiet secondary roads, and the impressive views along river valleys and from elevated viewpoints.

Q: Can you recommend a touring cycling route that explores the region's cultural heritage?
A: Certainly. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e917381109/tour-of-the-monasteries-of-la-safor-and-la-vall-d-albaida" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the Monasteries of La Safor and La Vall d'Albaida&lt;/a&gt; is a popular and challenging 23.7-mile (38.1 km) route specifically designed to explore the rich cultural heritage of the region, including the famous Monastery of San Jeronimo de Cotalba.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Rótova?
A: Yes, Rótova offers several easy routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33780390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mural on facade loop from Marxuquera Baixa&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 9.5-mile (15.3 km) route with minimal elevation gain, providing a gentle introduction to cycling in the area.
tips:
The Hieronymite Monastery of Sant Jeroni de Cotalba
was one of the most important monasteries in Valencia in the Middle Ages, belonging to the Hieronymite Order.

One of the best preserved monasteries in the Valencian Community. It is part of the long-distance route GR-236 known as the Route of the Monasteries. A site very rich in its history and significance in the area that make it worth visiting.
